step_by_step_thinking:
The articular disc of the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) has several distinctive features. It's indeed thickest posteriorly, which helps to guide mandibular movement during opening and closing; it narrows centrally creating distinct anterior and posterior portions that facilitate various movements like protrusion and retrusion; and importantly, its structure includes attachments from muscles such as the lateral pterygoid enhancing its stability. 

Therefore, my answer is D: All of the above. Each feature mentioned accurately describes aspects of the articular disc anatomy relevant for TMJ function. A B C all contribute unique characteristics but together they provide a comprehensive description of this anatomical component.
raw_response:
The articular disc of the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) has several distinctive features. It's indeed thickest posteriorly, which helps to guide mandibular movement during opening and closing; it narrows centrally creating distinct anterior and posterior portions that facilitate various movements like protrusion and retrusion; and importantly, its structure includes attachments from muscles such as the lateral pterygoid enhancing its stability. 

Therefore, my answer is D: All of the above. Each feature mentioned accurately describes aspects of the articular disc anatomy relevant for TMJ function. A B C all contribute unique characteristics but together they provide a comprehensive description of this anatomical component. Answer: D.
